# Trip Energy Consumption

> Calculates energy consumed per trip from battery range delta and a per-vehicle efficiency factor derived statistically from charging history and updated after each qualifying session.

**Category:** Asset · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** vehicle · energy · efficiency · ev · telemetry · trip

## What this does

Calculates energy consumed per trip from battery range delta and a per-vehicle efficiency factor derived statistically from charging history and updated after each qualifying session.

Specifies 3 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **trip_id** *(hidden, optional)* — Trip ID
- **vehicle_id** *(text, required)* — Vehicle ID
- **start_range_km** *(number, required)* — Start Range (km)
- **end_range_km** *(number, required)* — End Range (km)
- **range_delta_km** *(number, optional)* — Range Consumed (km)
- **preferred_range_metric** *(select, optional)* — Preferred Range Metric
- **vehicle_efficiency_wh_per_km** *(number, optional)* — Vehicle Efficiency (Wh/km)
- **estimated_energy_kwh** *(number, optional)* — Estimated Energy Consumed (kWh)

## What must be true

- **energy_from_range_and_efficiency:** Energy consumed per trip is estimated as range_delta_km x efficiency_wh_per_km divided by 1000
- **preferred_range_configurable:** Range delta uses the configured preferred_range_metric (ideal or rated); changing this setting affects all future efficiency derivations
- **efficiency_from_charging_history:** The efficiency factor is a per-vehicle value derived from the statistical mode of kWh-per-range-gained across qualifying charging sessions — not a fixed manufacturer specification
- **charging_session_qualifies:** A session qualifies for efficiency derivation only when duration > 10 minutes, end_battery_level <= 95%, both start and end range values are present, and charge_energy_added > 0
- **precision_fallback_strategy:** Derivation tries decreasing decimal precision (5 to 2) paired with decreasing minimum sample counts (8, 5, 3, 2) and accepts the first precision level with a consensus
- **retain_existing_if_no_consensus:** If no precision level yields a consensus the existing efficiency factor is retained unchanged and never deleted or set to null
- **no_negative_energy:** Negative range_delta (battery charged during trip via regeneration artifact) is treated as zero energy consumed

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Energy Estimated At Trip Close** — when trip has just completed; start_range_km exists; end_range_km exists; vehicle_efficiency_wh_per_km exists, then Energy estimate attached to the completed trip record.
- **Efficiency Updated After Charge** — when a qualifying charging session has just completed; at least 2 sessions meet the derivation criteria at some precision level, then Vehicle efficiency factor updated; future trip energy estimates improve.
- **Efficiency Unchanged Insufficient Data** — when a charging session has just completed; no consensus found across all precision and threshold levels, then Efficiency factor unchanged; previous value used for future estimates.

## Errors it can return

- `EFFICIENCY_NO_DATA` — Not enough qualifying charging sessions to derive an efficiency factor.

## Events

**`trip.energy.calculated`** — Energy consumption estimate attached to a completed trip
  Payload: `vehicle_id`, `trip_id`, `estimated_energy_kwh`, `range_delta_km`, `efficiency_wh_per_km`

**`vehicle.efficiency.updated`** — Vehicle efficiency factor recalculated from charging history
  Payload: `vehicle_id`, `new_efficiency_wh_per_km`, `sample_count`, `precision_used`

**`vehicle.efficiency.unchanged`** — Efficiency derivation ran but found no consensus; value kept
  Payload: `vehicle_id`, `reason`

## Connects to

- **vehicle-trip-segmentation** *(required)*
- **ev-charging-session** *(required)*
- **vehicle-efficiency-metrics** *(extends)*
